boundary: metadata only. no PII. no URLs, no filenames, no queries, no chat content. handle is opaque (alpha-NNN). enforced by `recall alpha review` and the welcome pack.

field_definitions.handle: opaque cohort id, e.g. alpha-001. never the person's real name or email.
field_definitions.install_date: YYYY-MM-DD, the day they ran the installer. null until they install.
field_definitions.platform: one of: windows-11, windows-10, macos, linux. coarse OS tag only.
field_definitions.browser: one of: chrome, edge, brave, arc, firefox, other. extension target browser.
field_definitions.used_days: integer count of days the daemon saw any event after install_date. derived, not self-reported.
field_definitions.recoveries_seen: integer count of recovery candidates the launcher rendered for them. local to their machine; reported by hand.
field_definitions.recoveries_used: integer subset of recoveries_seen they actually clicked Continue on.
field_definitions.wow: boolean. did they leave at least one quote in alpha/wow/?
field_definitions.failure: boolean. did they file at least one report in alpha/failures/?
field_definitions.status: one of: invited, installed, active, paused, dropped, completed.

users._note: founder; dogfood baseline. recoveries_seen=0 reflects the conservative recovery gate on a passive-browsing-heavy log. failure=true via the BUG-001 8B regression caught during 8C.

_provenance.founder_baseline: alpha-001 is the founder's own dogfood install on the dev box. The 4 other cohort slots are open as of 8E close; recruitment is the Phase 8F task.
_provenance.gap: 4 users short. 3 recoveries short. 1 wow short. failure target met (BUG-001).
